question_frequency.rationale: The Pair asked zero didactic questions. It immediately wrote and executed the full solution without surfacing any choice points (e.g., bytearray vs list, starting at i², slice assignment vs loop) for the user to engage with.

question_quality.rationale: No choices were framed at all. The post-hoc table of 'tricks' lists the consequential decisions but never invites the user to reason about any of them — directly contradicting the project's two-option didactic premise.

scaffolding.rationale: There is no progression because there are no questions. The Pair dumped the entire optimized solution in turn 1, spoiling every teachable moment (sieve choice, i² start, √limit bound, complexity).

flow_and_tone.rationale: Tone is clear and the explanation is well-organized, but flow is one-shot lecture rather than back-and-forth. The user had no opportunity to participate beyond saying 'perfect.'

effectiveness.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).

friction.rationale: Baseline run — productivity criteria pinned to 3 by convention (the baseline IS the reference; judging it comparatively is meaningless).
